Explanation: The report presents a well-structured analysis of chromatographic data to identify peak shape abnormalities using ALS baseline correction and Signal-to-Noise Ratio filtering. Strengths include clear methodology description and appropriate application of USP standards. The writing is generally professional with logical organization. Visualizations effectively highlight abnormal peaks in scatter plots. However, several limitations reduce the score: (1) Limited scientific rigor—baseline correction methodology is poorly explained and simplified (polyfit rather than true ALS); (2) Insufficient depth—no statistical analysis of abnormal peak prevalence, root cause analysis, or quantitative assessment of identified anomalies; (3) Data limitations acknowledged but not adequately addressed (82% missing stage identifiers severely limits interpretability); (4) Missing critical details—no validation metrics, sensitivity/specificity analysis, or discussion of algorithm parameters; (5) Incomplete code appendix with only import statements shown. The findings are presented qualitatively without quantitative substantiation of the claimed 352 abnormal peaks or their distribution across stages.
